content:
- verified the API/CLI/debug/docs slice for restore-control-plane references; the owned files were already clean of /restore and /ha/restore routes.
- added a node-api doc note that restore bootstrap is not a mutable node API surface, and tightened the debug verbose API-contract test to assert the endpoint inventory does not reintroduce restore routes.
- targeted verification passed with `cargo test debug_verbose_route_returns_structured_json_and_since_filter` and `cargo test --test bdd_api_http bdd_api_debug_routes_expose_ui_and_verbose_contracts -- --exact`; next hand this slice back to the parent so it can integrate with the DCS/HA removals.

- Checked the owned HA files first and found the restore control-plane logic itself was already gone from src/ha/actions.rs, src/ha/decide.rs, src/ha/worker.rs, and Makefile in the current branch.
- The remaining restore-era residue in my slice was dead backup harness plumbing in src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs. I removed ClusterFixture::start_with_backup, inlined the plain-cluster config into ClusterFixture::start, and forced backup: None there.
- That change made src/test_harness/ha_e2e/mod.rs re-export BackupHarnessConfig unnecessarily, so I removed the unused re-export to keep the build warning-free.
- Verification: env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/pgtusk-worker-ha cargo test --all-targets ha::decide::tests::transition_matrix_cases -- --exact (pass); env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/pgtusk-worker-ha cargo test --all-targets ha::worker::tests::dispatch_actions_maps_dcs_and_process_requests -- --exact (pass); env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/pgtusk-worker-ha cargo test --all-targets ha::e2e_multi_node::unit_tests::dual_primary_sample_assertion_passes_with_single_primary -- --exact (pass).
- I also triggered earlier parallel cargo test attempts on the default target dir; two of those hit shared-target/archive race errors and one passed. The dedicated target-dir reruns above are the clean verification signal to trust.
- If another agent later reintroduces backup/restore harnessing into HA e2e, they should do it intentionally in the harness layer rather than through dead optional wiring on ClusterFixture.

- Verified the DCS restore-control-plane slice is already absent on this branch.
- Checked src/dcs/state.rs, keys.rs, store.rs, worker.rs, and etcd_store.rs: no RestorePhase, RestoreRequestRecord, RestoreStatusRecord, restore_request/restore_status cache fields, or restore key/value handling remain.
- Ran make check successfully. Ran make test and it timed out in the default HA suite after 120s inside test.exec_default_suite; failure looked unrelated to the DCS slice and involved repeated start_postgres retries / pginfo poll failed churn in HA real-binary scenarios.
- User asked for a status-only report if DCS was already absent, so stop here and report the findings without making a no-op patch.

- Inspected src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs for the switchover stress path and traced request_switchover_via_cli -> cli::run -> CliApiClient::post_switchover -> POST /switchover.
- Confirmed ClusterFixture::control_node_index always returns 0, so the test hard-codes the first node only as the HTTP submission endpoint.
- Confirmed server semantics in src/api/controller.rs and src/api/worker.rs: POST /switchover validates the request body and writes /{scope}/switchover into DCS; no leader/self-node affinity. Conclusion: the clean fix is to stop pinning requests to node 0 and instead try any reachable API endpoint, with the caveat that reachability should really mean request acceptance / DCS-write success, not only GET /ha/state success.
